definitions: The process by which a continuous signal is sampled, and the resulting discrete signal contains frequencies that were not present in the original continuous signal.
The appearance of jagged or stair-stepped lines in digital images or graphics, caused by the discrete nature of pixels.
The situation where two or more different names or identifiers refer to the same underlying entity or memory location.
examples: The engineer was concerned about aliasing in the audio recording.
Graphics designers often use anti-aliasing techniques to reduce aliasing.
In programming, aliasing can lead to unexpected side effects.
